title: Accessing Pulse Widget data via APIs
excerpt: We are using pulse-level widgets on our Monday board.  One widget we have is named “Scope” (for the scope of work to be done for the associate pulse).  We need a way to access the value of this “Scope” widget in the puls…

posts.author: tom-csg (Tom Metz)
posts.cookedHtml: We are using pulse-level widgets on our Monday board. One widget we have is named “Scope” (for the scope of work to be done for the associate pulse). We need a way to access the value of this “Scope” widget in the pulse via API call. I don’t see any way to do that. Is it possible? If not, any creative ideas? Perhaps even outbound webhook calls that might include this data in the payload?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @tom-csg and welcome to the community!

Can you share a screenshot of your feature? I am not sure which one you are referring to. Also, what would the “value” of the scope be exactly?

Looking forward to hearing from you

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: tom-csg (Tom Metz)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Matias.Monday ! Thanks for your message. I thought for sure I had responded a day or so after your last message, but seems it didn’t save or something. Anywho… To answer your question. Please see this linked screenshot. This data is important to us via our APIs because we want our AI tool to do some analysis on it and trigger one of several different workflows based on that analysis. We can get to so many other fields related to this pulse, it seems like a gap to not have this data available in the APIs. Thanks!

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello again @tom-csg, there seems to be an issue with the image. It is not available to see (it says there is an issue).

Can you please send it again?
